title: Physolophium saxatile
intro: Physospermum saxatile, also known as Physospermum, is a perennial plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small white flowers and lance-shaped leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Physospermum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Physospermum is commonly prepared as a tincture, decoction, or powder, and is used in traditional herbal medicine to treat a range of conditions, including digestive issues and fever reduction.
